Thermal Management

Choosing Thermal Interface Materials for High-Density Electronics

July 1, 2026

This article is a draft placeholder — full content coming soon.

Datasheets list thermal conductivity under ideal lab conditions. Real mounting pressure, gap tolerance and production-line temperatures almost never match that. This piece will walk through how we actually choose between gap pads, thermal grease and phase-change materials for a given application — and where teams typically get it wrong.

What we'll cover

  • How mounting pressure changes effective thermal resistance
  • Gap pad vs. grease vs. phase-change: when each one actually wins
  • Why the datasheet's "typical" number is rarely what you'll see in production
← Back To Knowledge
